payload.audience: application developers using dali-ui public APIs

content_quality.default_object_model: Use Dali::Ui::View rather than Dali::Actor for app-facing object examples.
content_quality.guide_assumption: The reader is using dali-ui to build applications.

code_verification: receiver method exists on the receiver type
static methods are called statically and member methods are called on valid instances
assignment target is compatible with the method return type
argument count, argument type, and overload shape match the public API
enum owner and enum value both exist
property constants use the correct owner and value type
signal callback signatures match the public signal typedef
code blocks do not rely on inferred namespaces or overloads from naming alone
draft_requirements: Write enough practical detail for application developers to use the feature in a dali-ui app.
Ground natural-language behavior claims in the actual public headers, implementation files, samples, or UTCs when the context pack is not enough.
Before writing examples, inspect the referenced public headers and relevant sample files when needed.
Prefer examples copied or adapted from existing samples when they match the feature; otherwise keep examples small and verify every API use.
After drafting, review the completed Markdown line by line against repository evidence and fix inaccurate explanations or code before returning.
repair_requirements: Repair only the problematic spans and the smallest surrounding context needed; do not rewrite the whole document.
For each listed issue, use the repository evidence and context pack to verify the corrected wording or code before returning.
If an issue reveals the same API misuse nearby, fix the nearby equivalent misuse too; do not make unrelated style rewrites.
After repair, re-check the edited lines against public headers, implementation files, samples, or UTCs when needed.

rules: Use dali-ui symbols as the app-facing API surface.
Use dali-core and dali-adaptor symbols only as setup/background unless the usage pattern explicitly marks them preferred.
Prefer View-based examples over raw Actor object-model examples.

usage_patterns.intent: Create and configure the primary object using public dali-ui APIs.
usage_patterns.usage_note: Prefer typed constructors and setters when available.

usage_patterns.intent: React to user or resource events through public signals.
usage_patterns.usage_note: Use signal connection APIs only with their owning trait or class.

rendered_prompt:
You are reviewing an existing DALi dali-ui application developer guide draft.
This is Phase 2.3.5: source-grounded prose review.

Primary goal:
- Improve natural-language accuracy and guide flow using repository evidence.
- Preserve the document's useful structure and examples whenever possible.
- Do not perform a full rewrite.
- Keep the revised user-facing guide in en only.
- Remove Korean, Japanese, Chinese, or other non-English prose from the revised guide.

Feature target:
- Feature: Label (label)
- Category: views-components
- Guide profile: dali-ui-app
- Audience: application developers using dali-ui public APIs

Review rules:
- Review every natural-language paragraph line by line against public headers, implementation files, samples, and UTCs when needed.
- Accuracy is the first priority. Flow is the second priority.
- Edit the smallest unit needed: word, sentence, paragraph, or section-local addition/removal.
- If a word-level change is enough, change only the word.
- If a sentence-level change is enough, change only the sentence.
- If an entire paragraph is based on a wrong premise, revise, add, or remove that paragraph.
- Preserve code blocks when possible.
- If prose can be corrected to match an already accurate code block, prefer changing prose.
- If the code block itself makes the prose inaccurate or breaks the guide flow, rewrite only the smallest necessary part of that code block and verify it against public headers/source.
- Do not invent APIs.
- Do not add generic filler just to make the document longer.
- Do not change section structure unless prose_review.allow_section_restructure is true or the current prose is clearly misleading.
- Prefer localized edits when prose_review.localized_edits_only is true.
- Preserve code blocks especially when prose_review.preserve_code_blocks is true.
- Prefer Dali::Ui::View, typed setters, fluent chaining, and view-tree idioms for app-facing guidance.
- Treat dali-core and dali-adaptor APIs as reference/context unless the feature explicitly requires them.

Context summary:
{
  "content_quality": {
    "overview_style": "very_short",
    "require_table_of_contents": true,
    "require_rich_api_examples": true,
    "require_backticks_for_api_names": true,
    "topic_sections_must_be_feature_specific": true,
    "avoid_mechanical_api_listing": true,
    "default_object_model": "Use Dali::Ui::View rather than Dali::Actor for app-facing object examples.",
    "guide_assumption": "The reader is using dali-ui to build applications."
  },
  "prose_review": {
    "enabled": true,
    "apply_to": "all",
    "preserve_code_blocks": true,
    "localized_edits_only": true,
    "allow_section_restructure": false,
    "output_review_report": true,
    "fail_open_on_parse_error": true
  },

      "Prefer View-based examples over raw Actor object-model examples."
    ],

---
title: Label
sidebar_label: Label
category: views-components
---

# Label

`Dali::Ui::Label` displays non-editable text in a dali-ui `Dali::Ui::View` tree.

## Table of Contents

- [Create a Label as a View](#create-a-label-as-a-view)
- [Configure Font and Text Metrics](#configure-font-and-text-metrics)
- [Align Text and Resolve Direction](#align-text-and-resolve-direction)
- [Scale Text for Readability](#scale-text-for-readability)
- [Use Anchor Styling and Click Signals](#use-anchor-styling-and-click-signals)
- [Render Text Asynchronously](#render-text-asynchronously)
- [Use Label Property Constants](#use-label-property-constants)

## Create a Label as a View

`Dali::Ui::Label` is a `Dali::Ui::View`, so application code can create a label and pass it through the same view-based composition path used by other dali-ui components. Use `Dali::Ui::Label::New` with initial UTF-8 text when the label content is known at creation time.

```cpp
Dali::Ui::Label title = Dali::Ui::Label::New("Account settings");

title
  .SetFontFamily("Sans")
  .SetFontSize(28.0f)
  .SetFontWeight(Dali::Ui::Text::FontWeight::BOLD);

Dali::Ui::View titleView = title;
```

Use `Dali::Ui::Label::GetText`, `Dali::Ui::Label::GetFontFamily`, and `Dali::Ui::Label::GetFontSize` when application state needs to inspect the current label configuration.

```cpp
Dali::Ui::Label label = Dali::Ui::Label::New("Connected");

Dali::String text       = label.GetText();
Dali::String family     = label.GetFontFamily();
float        fontSizePx = label.GetFontSize();
```

## Configure Font and Text Metrics

The typed setters on `Dali::Ui::Label` return `Dali::Ui::Label&`, so they can be chained when configuring a component. `Dali::Ui::Label::SetFontSize` uses pixel units, while `Dali::Ui::Label::SetLineHeight` uses the interpretation configured for the label line-height mode.

```cpp
Dali::Ui::Label body = Dali::Ui::Label::New("A concise message for the current screen.");

body
  .SetFontFamily("Sans")
  .SetFontSize(18.0f)
  .SetFontWeight(Dali::Ui::Text::FontWeight::NORMAL)
  .SetFontWidth(Dali::Ui::Text::FontWidth::NORMAL)
  .SetFontSlant(Dali::Ui::Text::FontSlant::NORMAL)
  .SetLineHeight(1.4f);

float fontSize   = body.GetFontSize();
float lineHeight = body.GetLineHeight();
```

Use `Dali::Ui::Text::FontWeight`, `Dali::Ui::Text::FontWidth`, and `Dali::Ui::Text::FontSlant` to express font style without using stringly typed font properties.

```cpp
Dali::Ui::Label emphasis = Dali::Ui::Label::New("Important");

emphasis
  .SetFontSize(20.0f)
  .SetFontWeight(Dali::Ui::Text::FontWeight::SEMI_BOLD)
  .SetFontWidth(Dali::Ui::Text::FontWidth::NORMAL)
  .SetFontSlant(Dali::Ui::Text::FontSlant::ITALIC);

Dali::Ui::Text::FontWeight weight = emphasis.GetFontWeight();
Dali::Ui::Text::FontSlant  slant  = emphasis.GetFontSlant();
```

## Align Text and Resolve Direction

Use `Dali::Ui::Label::SetHorizontalTextAlignment` to position text within the label's own layout box. `Dali::Ui::Text::Alignment::START`, `Dali::Ui::Text::Alignment::CENTER`, and `Dali::Ui::Text::Alignment::END` are the alignment values exposed by the text API.

```cpp
Dali::Ui::Label heading = Dali::Ui::Label::New("Profile");

heading
  .SetFontSize(24.0f)
  .SetHorizontalTextAlignment(Dali::Ui::Text::Alignment::CENTER);

Dali::Ui::Text::Alignment alignment = heading.GetHorizontalTextAlignment();
```

For bidirectional or localized text, use `Dali::Ui::Label::SetLayoutDirectionMode`. `Dali::Ui::Text::LayoutDirectionMode::CONTENTS` resolves direction from the text content, `Dali::Ui::Text::LayoutDirectionMode::INHERIT` follows the parent view, and `Dali::Ui::Text::LayoutDirectionMode::LOCALE` follows the system locale.

```cpp
Dali::Ui::Label message = Dali::Ui::Label::New("Status");

message
  .SetFontSize(18.0f)
  .SetLayoutDirectionMode(Dali::Ui::Text::LayoutDirectionMode::LOCALE)
  .SetHorizontalTextAlignment(Dali::Ui::Text::Alignment::START);

Dali::Ui::Text::LayoutDirectionMode mode = message.GetLayoutDirectionMode();
```

## Scale Text for Readability

`Dali::Ui::Label::SetFontSizeScale` multiplies the configured font size. Use `Dali::Ui::Label::GetAdjustedFontSizeScale` to read the effective scale after minimum and maximum scale constraints are applied.

```cpp
Dali::Ui::Label readable = Dali::Ui::Label::New("Readable text");

readable
  .SetFontSize(18.0f)
  .SetFontSizeScale(1.2f);

float requestedScale = readable.GetFontSizeScale();
float adjustedScale  = readable.GetAdjustedFontSizeScale();
```

When an app wants to keep text within a readability range, configure the minimum and maximum font size scales with `Dali::Ui::Label::SetMinimumFontSizeScale` and `Dali::Ui::Label::SetMaximumFontSizeScale`.

```cpp
Dali::Ui::Label caption = Dali::Ui::Label::New("Caption");

caption
  .SetFontSize(14.0f)
  .SetFontSizeScale(1.5f)
  .SetMinimumFontSizeScale(0.8f)
  .SetMaximumFontSizeScale(2.0f);

float minimumScale = caption.GetMinimumFontSizeScale();
float maximumScale = caption.GetMaximumFontSizeScale();
float activeScale  = caption.GetAdjustedFontSizeScale();
```

## Use Anchor Styling and Click Signals

`Dali::Ui::Label` owns anchor color configuration through `Dali::Ui::Label::SetAnchorColor` and `Dali::Ui::Label::SetAnchorClickedColor`. These setters use `Dali::Ui::UiColor`, which can be constructed from direct RGBA components or a hex RGB value.

```cpp
Dali::Ui::Label linkText = Dali::Ui::Label::New("Open details");

linkText
  .SetFontSize(18.0f)
  .SetAnchorColor(Dali::Ui::UiColor(0x1A73E8))
  .SetAnchorClickedColor(Dali::Ui::UiColor(0x174EA6));

Dali::Ui::UiColor normalAnchor  = linkText.GetAnchorColor();
Dali::Ui::UiColor clickedAnchor = linkText.GetAnchorClickedColor();
```

Connect to `Dali::Ui::Label::AnchorClickedSignal` to receive anchor activation. The callback receives the owning `Dali::Ui::View` and the clicked `href` string.

```cpp
void OnAnchorClicked(Dali::Ui::View view, const Dali::String& href)
{
  Dali::Ui::Label source = Dali::Ui::Label::DownCast(view);
  if(source)
  {
    Dali::String currentText = source.GetText();
  }
}

Dali::Ui::Label label = Dali::Ui::Label::New("Documentation");

label.AnchorClickedSignal().Connect(&OnAnchorClicked);
```

## Render Text Asynchronously

`Dali::Ui::Label::SetAsyncRendering` enables asynchronous text rendering for relayout work that needs it. `Dali::Ui::Label::GetAsyncLineCount` returns the line count from the most recent asynchronous result.

```cpp
Dali::Ui::Label feedItem = Dali::Ui::Label::New("A longer item summary can be rendered asynchronously.");

feedItem
  .SetFontFamily("Sans")
  .SetFontSize(16.0f)
  .SetLineHeight(1.3f)
  .SetAsyncRendering(true);

int latestAsyncLines = feedItem.GetAsyncLineCount();
```

Use `Dali::Ui::Label::SetRenderScale` together with asynchronous rendering when text is visually scaled and needs higher rasterization quality. The render scale affects text rasterization quality, not the label layout size.

```cpp
Dali::Ui::Label zoomed = Dali::Ui::Label::New("Scaled label");

zoomed
  .SetFontSize(22.0f)
  .SetAsyncRendering(true)
  .SetRenderScale(2.0f);

float renderScale = zoomed.GetRenderScale();
```

## Use Label Property Constants

Prefer typed setters such as `Dali::Ui::Label::SetFontSize`, `Dali::Ui::Label::SetFontFamily`, and `Dali::Ui::Label::SetAsyncRendering` in application code. When a binding layer or metadata system needs property identifiers, use the constants owned by `Dali::Ui::Label::Property`.

```cpp
int textProperty        = Dali::Ui::Label::Property::TEXT;
int fontSizeProperty    = Dali::Ui::Label::Property::FONT_SIZE;
int fontFamilyProperty  = Dali::Ui::Label::Property::FONT_FAMILY;
int asyncProperty       = Dali::Ui::Label::Property::ASYNC_RENDERING;
int textColorProperty   = Dali::Ui::Label::Property::TEXT_COLOR;
int lineHeightProperty  = Dali::Ui::Label::Property::LINE_HEIGHT;
```

`Dali::Ui::Label::Property` also owns constants for layout, scaling, anchor, marquee, and rendering behavior, including `Dali::Ui::Label::Property::HORIZONTAL_ALIGNMENT`, `Dali::Ui::Label::Property::LAYOUT_DIRECTION_MODE`, `Dali::Ui::Label::Property::FONT_SIZE_SCALE`, `Dali::Ui::Label::Property::ANCHOR_COLOR`, `Dali::Ui::Label::Property::ANCHOR_CLICKED_COLOR`, `Dali::Ui::Label::Property::RENDER_SCALE`, and `Dali::Ui::Label::Property::PIXEL_SNAP_FACTOR`.

```cpp
int horizontalAlignmentProperty = Dali::Ui::Label::Property::HORIZONTAL_ALIGNMENT;
int layoutDirectionProperty     = Dali::Ui::Label::Property::LAYOUT_DIRECTION_MODE;
int fontScaleProperty           = Dali::Ui::Label::Property::FONT_SIZE_SCALE;
int anchorColorProperty         = Dali::Ui::Label::Property::ANCHOR_COLOR;
int renderScaleProperty         = Dali::Ui::Label::Property::RENDER_SCALE;
```
